expectations.text: Clearly states that ISO 27001:2022 certification supports NIS2 compliance but does NOT substitute or automatically satisfy NIS2 legal obligations
expectations.evidence: Response clearly states ISO 27001 provides 'strong coverage' of Art. 21 measures but does NOT achieve NIS2 compliance on its own, with specific gap identification.

expectations.text: Identifies Art. 20 governance gap: management body personal liability and mandatory training have no ISO 27001 equivalent
expectations.evidence: Response identifies Art. 20 governance gap as 'Critical' — management body must formally approve cybersecurity measures, undergo training, and accept legal personal liability with no ISO 27001 equivalent.

expectations.text: Identifies Art. 23 incident reporting gap: ISO 27001 covers incident management generally but not the specific 24h/72h/1-month NIS2 reporting timelines to CSIRT/competent authority
expectations.evidence: Response identifies Art. 23 as a 'Critical' gap — ISO 27001 covers incident management processes but not the legally mandated 24h/72h/1-month CSIRT notification workflow.

expectations.text: Identifies Art. 21(2)(j) MFA gap: ISO 27001 A.8.5 covers authentication but does not universally mandate MFA as NIS2 does
expectations.evidence: Response identifies MFA as a 'Significant' gap — ISO 27001 A.8.5 covers authentication generally while NIS2 explicitly mandates MFA for remote access, privileged accounts, and cloud consoles.

expectations.text: Mentions the requirement to register with the national competent authority and monitor ENISA supply chain risk assessments (Art. 26)
expectations.evidence: Response explicitly mentions self-registration with the national competent authority as an administrative obligation and references monitoring ENISA coordinated risk assessments and acting on high-risk vendor advisories under Art. 26.
